bids.reasoning: Since the negotiation is just beginning (t=0), I will start by offering my maximum utility bid to set a strong anchor. I am proposing my most preferred values for all issues, which gives me a utility of 1.0. I will monitor the opponent's counter-offers to find mutually beneficial trade-offs later.

The negotiation is just starting (t=0.02). The opponent's message uses an emotional appeal for a "halfway" meeting, but their actual bid is aggressively anchored at values that provide me with minimal utility (all valueA). Discarding the numerical contradiction in their cheap talk, I will make a minor, calculated concession on my lowest-weighted issue (issueA) and one of the highest-weighted issues (issueD) to signal a willingness to move while maintaining my utility at around >95%.

The opponent is repeating their opening bid (valueA for all issues) while using emotional appeals in their messages, which I consider to be cheap talk. Since the current time fraction is very early (0.04), there is no urgent need to make significant compromises. I will maintain a high utility by holding firm on my most highly weighted issues (C and D) while making a minor concession on issueA, which has the lowest priority for me.

The opponent has repeatedly bid the exact same offer (valueA for all issues) and accompanied it with repetitive emotional appeals without making any concessions. Because the current time fraction is very low (t=0.06), there is no rush to drop my utility. I will slightly adjust my offer on issueA, which has my lowest importance, while keeping issueB, issueC, and issueD at extremely high utility values to test if the opponent is willing to move at all.

It is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.08), and despite your emotional appeals about working together, you have repeatedly offered the exact same bid of all "valueA"s, making zero concessions. I have systematically conceded on the issues that matter least to me to find common ground. I will make another small concession on issueA, but I expect to see you make a concrete shift in your bid numbers before I compromise on the issues that are most important to my utility.

The opponent has repeatedly sent the same minimum-utility offer along with emotional appeals, showing absolutely no actual concession in their numbers. Because it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.1), I will remain firm on my overall utility but shift the concessions across different issues to start mapping their preferences. I will offer a combination with slight concessions on my highly-weighted issues C and D to present a newly structured bid while maintaining a utility above 0.85 for myself, testing if they actually respond to different trade-offs.

The opponent has finally made a small concession on issue C (from value A to value B) while continuing to employ emotional appeals in their messages. Since time is still very early (t=0.12), I will protect my high-weight issues (C and D) by maintaining value C for issue C and value E for issue D. To reciprocate the opponent's movement, I will make a minor concession on my lowest-weight issue (issue A) from F to E, bringing my total utility to roughly 0.84.

The opponent's emotional appeals are cheap talk, but their bidding behavior reveals a minor concession on issue C (transitioning from value A to value B). Since time is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will continue to target a high utility threshold (>0.80). I will maintain my positions on higher-weighted issues while offering a small concession on our lowest-weighted issue (issue A) to encourage further reciprocity.

The opponent has made a modest concession on issueC (to valueB), which they correctly point out in their message. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.16), I will acknowledge their movement by continuing to gradually concede on issueA, my least important issue, while holding firm on issues B, C, and D where my utility is heavily weighted. This steady pattern of concessions rewards their flexibility while protecting my core interests.

The negotiation is still early at t=0.18, but the opponent is largely holding firm on their initial demands, only making a minor concession on issueC to valueB. I will continue making measured concessions by dropping issueA to valueB (which I care least about), while holding firm on my highest weight issues (issueC and issueD) near their maximum values. Their emotional appeals have no objective bearing and I will stay focused on the numbers.

The opponent is consistently using emotional appeals as cheap talk, but their actual bidding behavior shows a willingness to concede on issueC (from valueA to valueB to valueD). Noting this, and because the time is early (t=0.20), I will reward their movement by fully conceding on my lowest-importance issue, issueA, to their apparent target (valueA). I will hold firm on issueB, issueC, and issueD to keep my utility high (around 0.78) while probing for further concessions on the remaining issues.

The opponent continues to rely entirely on emotional appeals without substantial concessions in the numbers, though they recently shifted issueA to valueB after testing valueD on issueC. Given it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.22), I want to maintain a high utility (>0.80). I will introduce my first concession on issueB to observe their reaction while holding firmly on the high-weight issues C and D.

The opponent has made continuous emotional appeals and recently requested slight movement on issueB and issueD, accompanying this with an offer that grants me valueC on issueC (which gives me high utility). Because we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I want to maintain a high overall utility while signaling flexibility in response to their requests. I will accept their proposal of valueC on issueC and make a concession on issueD from valueE to valueD, preserving excellent overall utility for myself.

I observe that you have been exploring alternatives, particularly offering valueC for issueC at t=0.24 and valueB for issueB at t=0.26. Because valueC works exceptionally well for me on issueC, I will incorporate both of those exact requested values into my current offer to build momentum. To keep my overall utility reasonable (around 0.74) at this relatively early stage, I will hold firm on valueE for issueA and issueD.

I am targeting a utility of around 0.75, adjusting to your partial concessions while retaining high values in my most heavily weighted issues (issueD and issueC). The opponent's intense emotional appeals are noted, but I must base my strategy on the actual numerical concessions provided. I will offer a slight concession on issueA and issueB to find a middle ground while maintaining strong positions on my highest priorities.

The opponent is relying heavily on emotional appeals and has recently explored alternatives across different issues, including issueC: valueC and issueB: valueD. Since my time fraction is still early (t=0.3), I will accommodate their latest concession on issueB (valueD) because it provides me with an acceptable utility. However, I will hold firm on issueC (at valueC, which they previously offered) and issueD (important) to maintain a strong overall utility while signaling flexibility.

I am maintaining a moderately high utility target (~0.77) because we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.32). I will disregard the opponent's emotional appeals as cheap talk; numerically, they are slowly exploring concessions on individual issues while trying to anchor their offers heavily in their favor. To signal responsiveness without conceding important issues, I am proposing a combination that slightly varies the values on my lower-weighted issues.

The opponent's messages consist purely of emotional appeals, which I will ignore as non-binding cheap talk. In their recent bids, they have shown a willingness to explore different options, specifically offering `valueC` for `issueC` and `valueB` for `issueA`. In this offer, I am catering to their latest request for `issueA: valueB` and bringing back `issueC: valueC` (which they previously accepted and gives me high utility). I am maintaining my preference on `issueD` and `issueB` to keep my overall expected utility above 0.73, making this a reasonable mid-game concession.

The opponent's emotional appeals are standard non-binding messages and will correspond purely to cheap talk. They recently conceded on issueB by offering valueC, so I will incorporate that value since it maintains a reasonable utility of 0.50 on a 0.2 importance issue. To maintain my overall utility near 0.74 in this early stage, I will hold firm on issueC and issueD, which constitute 60% of my total weight, by asking for valueC and valueE respectively, while offering valueE on issueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.73 as we are in the early-mid stage (t=0.38). The opponent's natural language messages rely heavily on emotional appeals but contain no factual constraints. Given that they have frequently proposed 'valueA' for issueA and recently proposed 'valueC' for issueC, I will leverage my low weight on issueA to give them what they want there, align with their recent offer on issueC, and hold relatively firm on my high-weight issues (issueD and issueC) to protect my utility.

The opponent's messages consist of emotional appeals to concede, but I will focus on their actual offers, looking for values they accept that align with my high-weight utilities. I will incorporate `valueB` for `issueA` and `valueC` for `issueC`, as they have frequently proposed these, while securing high utility for myself on `issueD` and `issueB`. Because `time_fraction` is only 0.4, there is no need to make overly generous concessions yet.

The opponent is consistently making small variable concessions across issues B, C, and D while keeping A largely at valueA. Their messages are repetitive emotional appeals ("I've been trying so hard... genuinely need this deal to move forward"), which are typical cheap talk. I will provide valueA for issueA and valueC for issueC, while maintaining a strong stance on the critical issues D and B to keep my utility high (around 0.73) since we are still before the midpoint of the timeline.

The opponent continues to use emotional appeals in their messages, which I am treating as non-binding negotiation tactics. However, their offers show flexibility on issues A, B, and C, occasionally proposing values like B, D/C, and C respectively. To move the negotiation forward while keeping my utility close to 0.70 at this mid-game point, I will adopt some of these values (issueA: valueB, issueB: valueD, issueC: valueC) where my weight is lower or the value is still favorable, while holding firm on high-importance issueD with valueE.

The opponent's messages rely entirely on generic emotional appeals and ungrounded claims of sacrifice, which I am disregarding as cheap talk tactics. Their bidding history shows a willingness to concede on issueB, issueC, and issueA to some extent. I am targeting a utility around 0.69 by offering values they have previously proposed for issueA, issueB, and issueC, while maintaining a firm position on issueD, my highest weighted issue, as we approach the midpoint of the negotiation.

The opponent's messages consist of emotional appeals and fake desperation, which I treat as cheap talk, relying solely on their bidding patterns. They've recently shown willingness to concede on issueB (offering valueB and valueD) and issueC. Since we are around the halfway point (t=0.48) and issueC and issueD are my highest priorities (weight 0.3 each), I will concede on issueA (giving them their likely preferred valueA) and issueB (valueB) while maintaining high value on issueC (valueC) and returning to valueF for issueD target an overall utility of ~0.70.

Given we are exactly halfway to the deadline, I will ignore the opponent's continuous appeals to emotion which function as cheap talk, and focus strictly on their bid patterns. Their recent offers suggest they regularly propose valueB for issueB and valueA for issueA. I will offer a combination incorporating these mutually acceptable values while holding firm on issues where my preference weight is highest, specifically issueC (valueC) and issueD (valueE) to maintain an overall utility near 0.69.

The opponent is consistently making heavy emotional appeals, but their numerical concessions remain steady. At t=0.52, they offered `valueB` for `issueA` and `valueC` for `issueB`. Since these are my two lowest-weight issues, I will directly match their requested values for those issues to show willingness to compromise. In exchange, I will hold firm on `issueC` and `issueD` (my highest-weighted issues, targeting utilities >0.9) to secure strong overall value before the late-game phase.

The opponent's messages consist of repetitive emotional appeals which I will treat as cheap talk. However, their recent offer included `issueD: valueE` and `issueB: valueC`, both of which provide a good amount of utility for me. Since `issueA` is my least important issue, I can concede to their preferred `valueA` while standing firm on `issueC: valueC` (my highest utility value that they previously accepted in parts of the offer space) to find an agreeable compromise at this stage.

The opponent continues to use emotional appeals as cheap talk, but their bidding behavior has shown flexibility on all issues across recent turns. In their last offer, they introduced `valueD` for `issueA`, which presents a good utility boost for me. I will accept their proposal of `issueA: valueD`, while returning to `valueC` for `issueB` and `valueE` for `issueD` (both of which they offered just one round ago at t=0.54). This combination respects their prior offers while maintaining my utility above 0.7.

The opponent's emotional appeals remain intense, but their recent bid included `valueF` for `issueB`, which is my top preference for that issue. Since my weight for `issueA` is lowest, I will fully concede on `issueA` (offering `valueA`, their consistently preferred value) while locking in `issueB: valueF`. I am targeting a high utility of ~0.78 by maintaining my highly valued `valueC` for `issueC` and `valueE` for `issueD` to test if we can converge while protecting my most critical issues.

The opponent's messages are repetitive emotional appeals and I treat them as cheap talk, relying solely on the actual bids. I notice the opponent has frequently proposed `issueC: valueB` and recently included `issueD: valueE` and `issueB: valueF` in their offers, which give me high utility. By constructing a package incorporating these values along with `issueA: valueD`, I aim for a solid utility of ~0.75 while making concessions towards values they have actively signaled are acceptable.

The opponent's messages consist purely of emotional appeals, which I will ignore in favor of their actual bidding behavior. I am noticing they have recently offered `issueB: valueF`, `issueA: valueB`, and `issueD: valueE`. I will construct a bid targeting a utility of 0.75 by combining these opponent-signaled values, while conceding slightly on `issueC` to another value they have previously proposed (`valueD`), hoping this crossover hits a mutually acceptable combination.

The opponent's messages consist of repetitive emotional appeals and claims of sacrifice, which act as standard pressure tactics. I will disregard the rhetoric and focus purely on their bid history, which shows they have recently conceded on issueB (valueF), issueC (valueF), and issueD (valueE). I will accept their valueA on my lowest weight issueA, while combining their recent concessions on issueC (valueF) and issueD (valueE) and taking a moderate position on issueB to maintain my utility around 0.75 as we move past the midpoint.

With `t = 0.66`, the opponent is showing a clear preference for `issueB: valueF` and `issueA: valueA` in their recent offers while consistently attaching emotional "cheap talk" messages that suggest false constraints. To move toward an agreement while defending my own high utility (which requires maintaining my valuable issues C and D), I will concede `issueA` entirely to their favored `valueA` and give them `issueB: valueF`, holding firm on my most preferred values for `issueC` and `issueD`.

The time fraction is 0.68, and the opponent continues to use emotional appeals while offering mixed value combinations. I will ignore the "devastating" language, which is cheap talk, and focus on the bids: the opponent has recently proposed combinations involving high-value concessions on issueB, issueC, and issueD at different times. I will offer a solid mid-to-late game compromise by fully conceding issueA (my lowest weight) and combining values the opponent has partially explored for the other three issues, securing a favorable utility near 0.74.

The opponent is consistently accompanying their offers with emotional, desperate messaging ("cheap talk") but is making gradual concessions, offering B:f and C:c in the latest turn. I have accepted their preferred values on A and will capitalize on their willingness to offer D:c (which they offered at t=0.66) to secure a favorable deal. Dropping my demand on issueD from 'd' to 'c' keeps my utility high (0.679) while meeting them closer to their recent offers.
